channel.title: The Cambridge Record — labor
channel.description: Everything on the record about labor.

item.title: POR 2026-108 — That the Cambridge City Council go on record expressing support for UNITE HERE Local 26 in the boycott of the Hyatt Rege
item.description: That the Cambridge City Council go on record expressing support for UNITE HERE Local 26 in the boycott of the Hyatt Regency Cambridge.

item.title: POR 2026-107 — S&S Restaurant “Thank You” Celebration
item.description: S&S Restaurant “Thank You” Celebration

item.title: POR 2026-75 — That the City Council go on the record urging Harvard administration to end the practice of time caps for non-tenure tra
item.description: That the City Council go on the record urging Harvard administration to end the practice of time caps for non-tenure track teaching faculty and urging Harvard administration to acknowledge the labor contribution and employee status of all its researchers, regardless of funding source, and contractua

item.title: POR 2026-66 — That the City Manager is requested to initiate the planning process for Cambridge 400 and as part of this, to convene a
item.description: That the City Manager is requested to initiate the planning process for Cambridge 400 and as part of this, to convene a Cambridge 400 Advisory Committee composed of members representing the range of stakeholders whose participation is needed to guide this work.

item.title: RES 2026-45 — Congratulations to the team behind Eastern Edge Food Hall on the opening of this new space in Kendall Square.
item.description: Congratulations to the team behind Eastern Edge Food Hall on the opening of this new space in Kendall Square.

item.title: POR 2026-43 — THAT THE CITY MANAGER BE AND HEREBY IS REQUESTED TO WORK WITH RELEVANT CITY DEPARTMENTS AS WELL AS THE ELECTION COMMISSI
item.description: THAT THE CITY MANAGER BE AND HEREBY IS REQUESTED TO WORK WITH RELEVANT CITY DEPARTMENTS AS WELL AS THE ELECTION COMMISSIONERS TO REVIEW GUIDANCE FROM THE IRS AND CONSULT WITH OTHER MUNICIPALITIES IN ORDER TO SIMPLIFY OUR PROCESSES FOR PAYING ELECTION WORKERS AND PAY WORKERS BY STIPEND WITHOUT MANDAT

item.title: POR 2026-36 — Policy Order Supporting Union Recognition at Lamplighter Brewing Co., with Appreciation for the Owners’ Community Contri
item.description: Policy Order Supporting Union Recognition at Lamplighter Brewing Co., with Appreciation for the Owners’ Community Contributions

item.title: RES 2026-15 — Resolution in Recognition of Michael J. Johnston, Esq., on his Retirement
item.description: Resolution in Recognition of Michael J. Johnston, Esq., on his Retirement

item.title: RES 2026-09 — Resolution on the Retirement of Kate Joyce
item.description: Resolution on the Retirement of Kate Joyce

item.title: POR 2025 #168 — That the City Council go on record in support of Lesley University faculty in their fight for a fair contract and urging
item.description: That the City Council go on record in support of Lesley University faculty in their fight for a fair contract and urging President Steinmayer and Lesley University administration to support a just contract for its workers

item.title: POR 2025 #158 — That this City Council go on record urging the Harvard administration to engage in good faith with the demands of worker
item.description: That this City Council go on record urging the Harvard administration to engage in good faith with the demands of workers represented by 32BJ SEIU, including fair pay, healthcare, retirement benefits, and improved protections for immigrant members

item.title: POR 2025 #155 — City Council support for the workers of Cambridge Rehabilitation & Nursing Center in their efforts to secure fair wages,
item.description: City Council support for the workers of Cambridge Rehabilitation & Nursing Center in their efforts to secure fair wages, respect, and improved working conditions through collective bargaining

item.title: POR 2025 #147 — City Council support of the unionization efforts of Massachusetts State House legislative staff and H.2093/S.1343, and u
item.description: City Council support of the unionization efforts of Massachusetts State House legislative staff and H.2093/S.1343, and urge the recognition of the Massachusetts State House Employee Union

item.title: POR 2025 #151 — City Council opposition to Harvard University’s efforts to strip union protections from workers in the Harvard Graduate
item.description: City Council opposition to Harvard University’s efforts to strip union protections from workers in the Harvard Graduate Students Union bargaining unit, and to all forms of union busting, and go on record urging the Harvard administration to engage in good faith with the demands of our residents orga

item.title: CMA 2025 #191 — A communication transmitted from Yi-An Huang, City Manager, relative to an update on Teamster strike and Republic Servic
item.description: A communication transmitted from Yi-An Huang, City Manager, relative to an update on Teamster strike and Republic Services

item.title: CMA 2025 #198 — A communication transmitted from Yi-An Huang, City Manager, relative to Awaiting Report Item Number 25-28, regarding an
item.description: A communication transmitted from Yi-An Huang, City Manager, relative to Awaiting Report Item Number 25-28, regarding an update on the status of potential civilian flagger operations in the Cambridge Police Union contract

item.title: CMA 2025 #213 — A communication transmitted from Yi-An Huang, City Manager, relative to Policy Order Number 25-101, regarding Marasao, e
item.description: A communication transmitted from Yi-An Huang, City Manager, relative to Policy Order Number 25-101, regarding Marasao, et al., Zoning Petition

item.title: CMA 2025 #206 — Transmitting Communication from Yi-An Huang, City Manager, relative to the appropriation of $500,000, from the General F
item.description: Transmitting Communication from Yi-An Huang, City Manager, relative to the appropriation of $500,000, from the General Fund Employee Benefits Salaries and Wages Account to the General Fund Employee Benefits Travel and Training Account. Funds will be used to cover the costs of anticipated payments of

item.title: ARS-25-21 — Awaiting Report Status Update 08.04.25
item.description: Awaiting Report Status Update 08.04.25

item.title: POR 2025 #101 — That the City Manager is requested to direct the City Solicitor to ensure that the wording for the proposed amendment to
item.description: That the City Manager is requested to direct the City Solicitor to ensure that the wording for the proposed amendment to Section 5.40 Footnote #2 is in line with the City Council’s intention to continue to include the inclusionary requirement for any nonreligious use property that is going above fou

item.title: ARS-25-20 — Awaiting Report Status Update 06.30.25
item.description: Awaiting Report Status Update 06.30.25

item.title: — POR 2025 #94 : That the City Council go on record expressing its profound disappointment over Apex Clean Energy’s deci
item.description: POR 2025 #94 : That the City Council go on record expressing its profound disappointment over Apex Clean Energy’s decision to forgo the use of local union labor on the Bowman Wind Project

item.title: POR 2025 #97 — That the Cambridge City Council urges Mass General Brigham to recognize the MGB Primary Care Physician union and immedia
item.description: That the Cambridge City Council urges Mass General Brigham to recognize the MGB Primary Care Physician union and immediately begin good faith negotiations

item.title: RES 2025 #148 — That the City Council go on record thanking SEIU 32BJ for their vigilance, partnership and advocacy on behalf of unioniz
item.description: That the City Council go on record thanking SEIU 32BJ for their vigilance, partnership and advocacy on behalf of unionized workers. CHARTER RIGHT EXERCISED BY MAYOR SIMMONS IN COUNCIL JUNE 16, 2025 PLACED ON THE TABLE IN COUNCIL JUNE 23, 2025

item.title: APP 2025 #22 — An application was received from Andrew Jawa, requesting permission for a curb cut at the premises numbered 11 Union Str
item.description: An application was received from Andrew Jawa, requesting permission for a curb cut at the premises numbered 11 Union Street; said petition has received approval from Inspectional Services, Traffic, Parking and Transportation, Historical Commission and Public Works. No response has been received fro

item.title: AR-25-28 — That the City Manager is requested to prepare an update with details on the status of potential civilian flagger operati
item.description: That the City Manager is requested to prepare an update with details on the status of potential civilian flagger operations in the Cambridge police union contract and work with relevant city staff to explore a civilian traffic flagger program and update the current police union contract on the City'

item.description: A communication transmitted from Yi-An Huang, City Manager, relative to Awaiting Report Item Number 25-16, regarding MBTA bus issues

item.description: That the City Manager is requested to prepare an update with details on the status of potential civilian flagger operations in the Cambridge police union contract and work with relevant city staff to explore a civilian traffic flagger program and update the current police union contract on the City'

item.description: A communication transmitted from Yi-An Huang, City Manager, relative to Awaiting Report Item Number 25-23 regarding a Citywide Shuttle Bus Pilot program

item.description: Late Resolution Re Whitney's Bar

item.description: Congratulations to SHARE CHA and its members on filing for union certification with the Massachusetts Department of Labor Relations; urging CHA management for a fair process, to recognize their bargaining unit without delay, and to begin good-faith negotiations expeditiously; and supporting the rati

item.description: A communication transmitted from Yi-An Huang, City Manager, relative to an update from the Human Resources Department

item.title: POR 2024 #134 — City Council support of a fair union contract for Cambridge Hospital residents and calls on CHA leadership to swiftly an
item.description: City Council support of a fair union contract for Cambridge Hospital residents and calls on CHA leadership to swiftly and thoughtfully settle a contract that reflects the value of the resident physicians, the patients they serve, and the crucial importance of Cambridge Hospital to our community

item.description: City Council support of the Act Establishing Protections and Accountability for TNC and DNC Workers (S.627/H1158), which would allow TNCs to sustain worker benefits and protections that maintain a level playing field across transportation industries. item.description: Congratulations to the Blue Bottle Independent Union

item.description: A communication transmitted from Yi-An Huang, City Manager, relative to Awaiting Report Item Number 2024 #06, regarding micromobility rules and regulations

item.description: Congratulations to Norma Wassel on being awarded the Lifetime Achievement Award by the National Association of Social Workers

item.description: A communication transmitted from Yi-An Huang, City Manager, relative to Policy Order # 23-133, regarding regarding possible projections of build-out of the Alewife Quadrangle under the proposed AOD-Q zoning petition

item.description: City Council support of graduate student workers of Northeastern University and their effort to form a union

item.description: Recognizing June 19, 2023 as Juneteenth in the City of Cambridge, and in urging all Cambridge residents to reflect upon the meaning of this important day

item.description: A communication transmitted from Yi-An Huang, City Manager, relative to an update on Afterschool care

item.description: Transmitting Communication from Yi-An Huang, City Manager, relative to the transfer of $325,000 in the General Fund between School Department statutory accounts as follows: $325,000 from the Salary and Wages account to the School Travel and Training account ($205,000) and to the School Extraordinar

item.title: RES 2023 #73 — That the City Council recognize March as National Social Workers Month
item.description: That the City Council recognize March as National Social Workers Month

item.title: ORD 2022 # 6 — An Ordinance has been received from Diane P. LeBlanc, relative to Ordinance # 2022-6 Article III Green Jobs 2.66.100 Def
item.description: An Ordinance has been received from Diane P. LeBlanc, relative to Ordinance # 2022-6 Article III Green Jobs 2.66.100 Definitions. PASSED TO A SECOND READING IN COUNCIL FEBRUARY 27, 2023 TO BE ORDAINED ON OR AFTER MARCH 20, 2023. ORDAINED IN COUNCIL MARCH 20, 2023

item.title: POR 2023 #39 — That the joint Economic Development and University Relations and Neighborhood and Long-Term Planning, Public Facilities,
item.description: That the joint Economic Development and University Relations and Neighborhood and Long-Term Planning, Public Facilities, Arts & Celebrations Committees forward the amended proposed Policy Order regarding Labs and Neighborhood Planning to the full City Council. CHARTER RIGHT EXERCISED BY COUNCILLOR Z

item.title: CMA 2023 #41 — A communication transmitted from Yi-An Huang, City Manager, relative to the appointment of the following persons as a me
item.description: A communication transmitted from Yi-An Huang, City Manager, relative to the appointment of the following persons as a members of the Foundry Advisory Committee for a term of three years: Connie Chin; Barbara Thomas; and Rubén Mancha

item.title: COF 2023 #14 — A Communication received from Naomie Stephen, Assistant to the Council transmitting consolidated list of questions
item.description: A Communication received from Naomie Stephen, Assistant to the Council transmitting consolidated list of questions

item.title: CMA 2022 #237 — A communication transmitted from Yi-An Huang, City Manager, relative to an amendment to the proposed Home Rule Petition
item.description: A communication transmitted from Yi-An Huang, City Manager, relative to an amendment to the proposed Home Rule Petition for a special act regarding a fire cadet program for the City of Cambridge Fire Department

item.title: POR 2022 #296 — That the Rules of the City Council, Rule 24B be amended to strike the words “not limited to” from the first sentence and
item.description: That the Rules of the City Council, Rule 24B be amended to strike the words “not limited to” from the first sentence and replace them with “no more than”

item.title: RES 2022 #289 — Resolution Re Harvard Clerical and Technical Workers Union
item.description: Resolution Re Harvard Clerical and Technical Workers Union

item.title: POR 2022 #288 — Revised MBTA Bus Redesign
item.description: Revised MBTA Bus Redesign

item.description: Transmitting Communication from Louis A. DePasquale, City Manager, relative to the transfer of $60,000 from the General Fund Traffic, Parking and Transportation Salary and Wages account to the General Fund Traffic, Parking and Transportation Travel and Training (Judgment and Damages) account to cove

item.title: POR 2022 #48 — City Council support of the MIT Graduate Student Union
item.description: City Council support of the MIT Graduate Student Union

item.title: POR 2022 #50 — City Council support urging the Massachusetts Legislature and the residents of Cambridge to oppose House Bill 1234 and t
item.description: City Council support urging the Massachusetts Legislature and the residents of Cambridge to oppose House Bill 1234 and the proposed 2022 state ballot initiative and ensuring that the rights of workers remain solid and strong for generations to come

item.title: CMA 2022 #34 — A communication transmitted from Louis A. DePasquale, City Manager, relative to the Climate Resilience Zoning Task Force
item.description: A communication transmitted from Louis A. DePasquale, City Manager, relative to the Climate Resilience Zoning Task Force Report. (Ordinance #2022-9)

item.title: POR 2021 #257 — That the City Council go on record strongly urging Harvard and its property service contractors to offer a fair contract
item.description: That the City Council go on record strongly urging Harvard and its property service contractors to offer a fair contract that ensures wage increases to the workers of 32BJ SEIU

item.title: POR 2021 #238 — That the Cambridge City Council go on record supporting the Harvard Graduate Student Union in their continued efforts to
item.description: That the Cambridge City Council go on record supporting the Harvard Graduate Student Union in their continued efforts to negotiate a fair contract that is worthy of the value they bring to Harvard University and the protections from harassment and discrimination, which all people deserve, and that t

item.title: POR 2021 #209 — City Council support of the MIT Graduate Student Union
item.description: City Council support of the MIT Graduate Student Union

item.title: CMA 2021 #215 — A communication transmitted from Louis A. DePasquale, City Manager, relative to a request for approval to submit an amen
item.description: A communication transmitted from Louis A. DePasquale, City Manager, relative to a request for approval to submit an amendment to the proposed Home Rule Petition for a special law regarding a fire cadet program for the City of Cambridge Fire Department

item.title: CMA 2021 #182 — A communication transmitted from Louis A. DePasquale, City Manager, relative to a recommendation of the Cambridge Histor
item.description: A communication transmitted from Louis A. DePasquale, City Manager, relative to a recommendation of the Cambridge Historical Commission to approve the final Landmark Designation Report for the Alberta V. Scott House at 28 Union Street

item.title: POR 2021 #124 — That the City Manager be and is hereby requested to work with the Director of the Public Libraries with input from the L
item.description: That the City Manager be and is hereby requested to work with the Director of the Public Libraries with input from the Library staff and Library Union to fully open the Main Library as well as the Branch libraries on or before June 15th to ensure that residents have access to the materials provided

item.title: POR 2021 #60 — That the Cambridge City Council go on record expressing its gratitude for Dr. West’s time in Cambridge, its disappointme
item.description: That the Cambridge City Council go on record expressing its gratitude for Dr. West’s time in Cambridge, its disappointment to see him leave the city, and wishing him the best of luck at Union Theological Seminary and in his future endeavors

item.title: POR 2021 #52 — That the City Manager be and hereby is requested to reimplement capacity restrictions on indoor dining to ensure the saf
item.description: That the City Manager be and hereby is requested to reimplement capacity restrictions on indoor dining to ensure the safety of restaurant workers, diners, and Cambridge residents until widespread vaccination of the general public is achieved in the coming weeks

item.title: — COM 120 #2021 : A communication was received from Carolyn Magid, regarding PO 2021 #36: Task Force Transparency PO 2021
item.description: COM 120 #2021 : A communication was received from Carolyn Magid, regarding PO 2021 #36: Task Force Transparency PO 2021#38: Shelter Wages.

item.description: ORDINANCE 2021-6: A Zoning Petition Has been received from Beals Associates Inc. regarding Broad Canal Subdistrict Zoning Petition as submitted with strike outs

item.title: POR 2020 #292 — That the Cambridge City Council calls upon the Animal Legal defense Fund management to respect the democratic choice of
item.description: That the Cambridge City Council calls upon the Animal Legal defense Fund management to respect the democratic choice of its workers by, without delay, voluntarily recognizing ALDF United and bargaining a contract with ALDF United that reflects the core values of ALDF — compassion, innovation, balanc

item.title: CMA 2020 #286 — A communication transmitted from Louis A. DePasquale, City Manager, relative to Awaiting Report Item Number 20-62, regar
item.description: A communication transmitted from Louis A. DePasquale, City Manager, relative to Awaiting Report Item Number 20-62, regarding providing interpreters at polling locations

item.title: AR-20-69 — Report on formulating an RFP for a public arts project that will acknowledge the unfinished work of the 19th Amendment,
item.description: Report on formulating an RFP for a public arts project that will acknowledge the unfinished work of the 19th Amendment, the importance of the Voting Rights Act of 1965, and how the two pieces of legislation ultimately complemented one another in helping to shape a more perfect union

item.title: POR 2020 #273 — That the City Council formally go on record in asking the City Manager to work with the appropriate City Staff in formul
item.description: That the City Council formally go on record in asking the City Manager to work with the appropriate City Staff in formulating an RFP for a public arts project that will acknowledge the unfinished work of the 19th Amendment, the importance of the Voting Rights Act of 1965, and how the two pieces of l

item.title: RES 2020 #277 — That the City Council go on record thanking the Cambridge Election Commission, for their tireless efforts this election
item.description: That the City Council go on record thanking the Cambridge Election Commission, for their tireless efforts this election season, and all of the poll workers in Cambridge for their dedication and service in ensuring everyone’s votes were counted

item.title: RES 2020 #262 — That the City Council go on record congratulating Andrea White on her historic appointment as President and CEO of Naveo
item.description: That the City Council go on record congratulating Andrea White on her historic appointment as President and CEO of Naveo Credit Union

item.title: CMA 2020 #230 — Transmitting Communication from Louis A. DePasquale, City Manager, relative to the appropriation of $5,000,000 from Free
item.description: Transmitting Communication from Louis A. DePasquale, City Manager, relative to the appropriation of $5,000,000 from Free Cash to the Public Investment Fund Executive Department Extraordinary Expenditures Account for the City's third annual contribution to the MBTA Green Line Extension project

item.description: That the City Council stand in solidarity with the SECSE workers striking at Good Vibrations

item.description: Report on providing an update on the Cambridge Police Patrol Officers’ Association union contract negotiation

item.description: A communication transmitted from Louis A. DePasquale, City Manager, relative to Awaiting Report Item Number 20-35, regarding an update on the Cambridge Police Patrol Officers' Association union contract negotiations

item.description: That the City Manager be and hereby is requested to confer with the Personnel Department and provide an update on the Cambridge Police Patrol Officers’ Association union contract negotiation at the City Council’s regular meeting on September 14th, 2020

item.description: A communication transmitted from Louis A. DePasquale, City Manager, relative to a Surveillance Technology Impact Report for electronic poll pads, requested by the Election Commission

item.title: RES 2020 #163 — That the City Council go on record congratulating the CRLS Black Student Union Graduating Class of 2020, in recognizing
item.description: That the City Council go on record congratulating the CRLS Black Student Union Graduating Class of 2020, in recognizing their strong advocacy of and commitment to social justice, and in recognizing the tremendous positive ripples these young adults will continue to create in the decades to come

item.description: That the City Council go on record in solidarity with all Lesley University workers and in support of the demands of the community petition

item.description: Sundry communications were received in support of Lesley University Workers

item.description: Congratulations to Rui Domingos on his new position as the CEO/President of the MIT Federal Credit Union

item.description: That the City Manager be and hereby is requested to establish what specific steps will be made to ensure the safety of the workforce upon the re-opening of City offices, and what specific options will be made available to those City workers who are more susceptible to severe covid-19 infections and

item.description: That the City Manager be and hereby is requested to confer with the Public Health Department to develop and implement stricter public health regulations to protect our grocery store workers

item.description: That the City Manager be and hereby is requested to light up City Hall blue to honor all frontline workers during the COVID-19 pandemic, Friday, April 17, 2020

item.description: That the City Council go on record thanking the Black Student Union for their important video thanking the Cambridge Rindge and Latin School's Custodial and Food Servicepersons for their dedicated service
